message:
🌱 gitea: Integrate or Interface Docker Registry (#1933)

* 🌱 Add gitea: Integrate or Interface Docker Registry mission

* 🌱 Add gitea: Integrate or Interface Docker Registry mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "gitea-2316-integrate-or-interface-docker-registry",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "gitea: Integrate or Interface Docker Registry",
+    "description": "Integrate or Interface Docker Registry. Requested by 118+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current gitea setup",
+        "description": "Verify your gitea version and configuration:\n```bash\ngitea --version\n```\nThis feature requires a working gitea installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review gitea configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant gitea configuration:\nI know, this may be completely out of scope for this project. But I still try:\n\nIt would be cool if gitea could provide support for a docker registry.\n\nFull integration is probably very unlikely to ever being added."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Integrate or Interface Docker Registry",
+        "description": "For third party authentication, they have various files in that authn directory that connect to various authentication providers. So there are various options, 1. complete #27 to add an Oauth provider to Gitea and then you have the GitHub provider in cesanta that you can re-use (this option\n```yaml\n{\n  \"admin\": true,\n  \"pull\": true,\n  \"push\": true\n}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Integrate or Interface Docker Registry\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "For third party authentication, they have various files in that authn directory that connect to various authentication providers. So there are various options, 1. complete #27 to add an Oauth provider to Gitea and then you have the GitHub provider in cesanta that you can re-use (this option requires work for both projects). Option 2.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "{\n  \"admin\": true,\n  \"pull\": true,\n  \"push\": true\n}",
+        "events {\n    worker_connections  1024;\n}\n\nhttp {\n  upstream docker-registry {\n    server registry:5000;\n  }\n  upstream auth-service {\n    server auth-service:80;\n  }\n\n  ## Set a variable to help us decide if we need to add the\n  ## 'Docker-Distribution-Api-Version' header.\n  ## The registry always sets this header.\n  ## In the case of nginx performing auth, the header is unset\n  ## since nginx is auth-ing before proxying.\n  map $upstream_http_docker_distribution_api_version $docker_distribution_api_version {\n    '' 'registry/2.0';\n  }\n\n  server {\n      listen 443 ssl;\n\n      # SSL\n      ssl_certificate /certs/domain.crt;\n      ssl_certificate_key /certs/domain.key;\n      # Recommendations from https://raymii.org/s/tutorials/Strong_SSL_Security_On_nginx.html\n      ssl_protocols TLSv1.1 TLSv"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "gitea",
+      "community",
+      "git-hosting",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "gitea"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/go-gitea/gitea/issues/2316",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/go-gitea/gitea"
+    },
+    "reactions": 118,
+    "comments": 30,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "tools": [
+      "docker",
+      "git"
+    ],
+    "description": "A working gitea installation or development environment."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-02T06:59:07.805Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
